chief_complaint: A 30-year-old man presents with a two-year history of recurrent symptoms including scleritis, fever, cough, auricular chondritis, rash, and transfusion-dependent macrocytic anemia.
history: The patient has a two-year history of recurrent scleritis, fever, cough, auricular chondritis, rash, and transfusion-dependent macrocytic anemia. Despite multiple immunosuppressants and broad-spectrum antimicrobials, symptoms persisted. He has also had relapsing bilateral ground-glass pulmonary infiltrates.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Imaging revealed relapsing bilateral ground-glass pulmonary infiltrates. Bone marrow showed vacuolated precursors and cytogenetic abnormalities. A somatic UBA1 mutation (NM_003334 exon3 c.121A>G p.M41V) was detected with high variant allele frequency in blood, marrow, and bronchoalveolar lavage cells.
correct_diagnosis: VEXAS syndrome with early myelodysplastic features
differentials: VEXAS syndrome with early myelodysplastic features
correct_ranking: VEXAS syndrome with early myelodysplastic features
